senses.definition: (of a place) able to be reached, entered, etc.

examples.x: The wheelchair ramp made the building more accessible to people with mobility impairments.
The online platform provides accessible resources for students to access course materials from anywhere.
The conference venue was chosen for its central location and accessible facilities for attendees.
The library has made efforts to ensure that its digital archives are accessible to researchers worldwide.
The hotel provides accessible rooms equipped with grab bars and widened doorways for guests with mobility challenges.

senses.definition: relating to or using signals in which information is represented through constant changing of physical quantities
having the ability to protect a system from viruses by finding and destroying them
having the ability to work with different devices, machines, etc.

examples.x: The new printer is compatible with both Windows and Mac operating systems, ensuring flexibility for users.
It's important to check whether your smartphone is compatible with the latest software updates to ensure optimal performance.
The game console supports various controllers, making it compatible with a wide range of gaming accessories.
The wireless headphones are compatible with multiple devices, including smartphones, tablets, and laptops, offering versatility for users on the go.
The new software update ensures that files created in the latest version are compatible with older versions of the program.

senses.definition: (of a computer system) not working temporarily or properly
describing the constant passage of data between a computer or other device and a user

examples.x: The educational software offers interactive lessons that engage students in active learning.
The video game provides an immersive interactive experience, allowing players to control characters and explore virtual worlds.
The website includes interactive elements such as quizzes and polls to engage visitors and gather feedback.
The interactive map allows users to zoom in and out, pan across different areas, and access additional information with a click.

senses.definition: the system through which different devices can be connected to each other over short distances wirelessly using radio waves

examples.x: The Bluetooth technology in my car allows me to connect my phone and stream music wirelessly while driving.
We used Bluetooth to transfer photos from our phones to the computer without needing any cables.
The new headphones come with Bluetooth connectivity, so you can listen to music without being tethered to your device.
Many modern smartwatches feature Bluetooth capability, enabling them to receive notifications from your smartphone.
The wireless speaker connects to my phone via Bluetooth, so I can play music from anywhere in the room.

senses.definition: a type of blue disk on which large data such as high-definition videos can be stored

examples.x: We watched the latest blockbuster movie on Blu-ray for the best audio and video quality.
I prefer to buy movies on Blu-ray because of the high-definition picture and superior sound quality.
The special edition box set includes all seasons of the TV series on Blu-ray, along with exclusive collectible items.

senses.definition: the technology or system for connecting other devices to a computer

examples.x: The printer connects to the computer via a USB cable for easy printing.
Many modern devices, such as smartphones and tablets, charge using a USB port.
The USB port on the laptop allows for quick and easy connection of external devices.
The USB hub provides additional ports for connecting multiple peripherals to the computer.

senses.definition: a unit for measuring the size of computer data that equals 8 bits

examples.x: The text file is 1 megabyte in size, which is equivalent to 8 million bytes.
The image file contains millions of bytes of data, making it high-resolution and detailed.
The program requires at least 4 gigabytes of RAM, which is equivalent to billions of bytes, to run smoothly.
The video file size is 2 gigabytes, consisting of approximately 16 billion bytes of information.
The music track is stored in MP3 format and is 4 megabytes in size, translating to around 32 million bytes.

senses.definition: a unit for measuring computer data that equals 1024 bytes

examples.x: The text document is only a few kilobytes in size, making it easy to send via email.
The image file is 500 kilobytes, containing detailed graphics suitable for web display.
The software update is 2 megabytes, comprising thousands of kilobytes of code improvements.
The spreadsheet file size is 1.5 megabytes, equivalent to approximately 1.5 million kilobytes of data.
The game download is 100 megabytes, consisting of approximately 100,000 kilobytes of gaming content.

senses.definition: a unit for measuring computer data that equals 1024 kilobytes

examples.x: The high-resolution image file is 5 megabytes in size, containing thousands of kilobytes of data.
The software update is 100 megabytes, which is equivalent to over 100,000 kilobytes of code.
The video file size is 50 megabytes, comprising millions of bytes of visual and audio information.
The music album download is 80 megabytes, containing approximately 80,000 kilobytes of audio data.
The game requires at least 500 megabytes of storage space on your device, which is equivalent to half a million kilobytes.

senses.definition: a unit for measuring computer data that equals 1024 megabytes

examples.x: The hard drive has a storage capacity of 1 terabyte, equivalent to 1024 gigabytes of data.
The movie download is 5 gigabytes in size, containing thousands of megabytes of high-definition video.
The smartphone comes with 128 gigabytes of internal storage, allowing users to store millions of photos and songs.
The video game requires at least 50 gigabytes of free space on your computer's hard drive for installation.
The cloud storage service offers a plan with 1 gigabyte of storage space for free, with options to upgrade to larger capacities.

senses.definition: a unit for measuring computer data that equals 1024 gigabytes

examples.x: The external hard drive can store up to 4 terabytes of data, providing ample space for backups and archives.
The data center has a storage capacity of several petabytes, each equivalent to 1024 terabytes of data.
The movie collection occupies 2 terabytes of storage space, containing thousands of gigabytes of high-definition films.
The university's research project requires a storage capacity of 10 terabytes to analyze and store massive datasets.
The high-definition video project required 3 terabytes of storage space to accommodate the large files.

senses.definition: (computing) a copy of computer data that can be used to restore lost or damaged data
the keeping of data on a computer, etc.

examples.x: Cloud storage services allow users to access their files from anywhere with an internet connection.
The company upgraded its storage infrastructure to accommodate the increasing amount of data generated by its operations.
Flash drives provide portable storage solutions for transferring files between computers.
Data storage capacity has increased exponentially over the years, enabling individuals and businesses to store vast amounts of information digitally.
The smartphone comes with ample internal storage for storing apps, photos, and videos.

senses.definition: a small device used for storing data or transferring data between electronic devices

examples.x: I transferred the presentation to a flash drive so that I could easily share it with my colleagues.
The flash drive contains important documents and photos that I need for my trip.
Many students use flash drives to store their assignments and projects for easy access at school.
The flash drive is small enough to fit in your pocket, making it convenient for carrying data on the go.
The IT department distributed flash drives to employees for backing up their work files and documents.

senses.definition: a disk on which data is stored, either inside or outside a computer

examples.x: The computer's hard disk drive is running out of space due to the large amount of data stored on it.
I installed a new hard disk drive in my laptop to increase its storage capacity.
The company's server room is equipped with multiple hard disk drives to store and manage its vast amount of data.
External hard disk drives are popular for backing up important files and documents.
The technician replaced the faulty hard disk drive in the desktop computer to resolve the data corruption issues.

senses.definition: the non-physical space in which communication over computer networks takes place

examples.x: With the rise of social media platforms, interactions in cyberspace have become increasingly common.
Online forums provide a platform for discussions and exchanges of ideas in cyberspace.
The cybersecurity team monitors cyberspace for potential threats and unauthorized access attempts.
Cyberspace presents both opportunities and challenges for businesses looking to expand their online presence.
The government has implemented regulations to ensure the safety and security of citizens in cyberspace.

senses.definition: a set of actions done on data by a computer to have a certain result

examples.x: The company invested in advanced data processing software to streamline its operations and improve efficiency.
Data processing involves collecting, organizing, and analyzing large volumes of information to extract meaningful insights.
The data processing center is responsible for managing and maintaining the organization's databases and systems.
Modern businesses rely on data processing technologies to make informed decisions and drive strategic initiatives.
The course covers various techniques and methods used in data processing to prepare students for careers in data analytics and information management.

senses.definition: a predefined option based on which a computer or other device performs a particular task unless it is changed

examples.x: The email client automatically sends messages to the "Spam" folder by default unless the user specifies otherwise.
The default setting for the browser's homepage is the search engine's website, but users can change it to their preferred page.
The smartphone's default wallpaper is a scenic landscape, but users can customize it with their own photos.
The software's default font size is 12 points, but users can adjust it to their preference in the settings.
The default language on the operating system is English, but users can switch to other languages if needed.

senses.definition: a movable mark on a computer screen that indicates where the user is working

examples.x: Move the cursor to the desired location and click to select.
The cursor blinked steadily on the blank screen, waiting for input.
She moved the cursor across the document to highlight the relevant text.
If the cursor freezes, try restarting the computer to resolve the issue.
You can change the appearance of the cursor in the system settings.

senses.definition: an electronic device that visually presents data

examples.x: The store had a wide selection of displays, ranging from high-definition monitors to large-screen televisions.
The museum installed interactive displays to engage visitors with historical information.
The dashboard display on the car showed the current speed, fuel level, and navigation instructions.
The conference room was equipped with a large display for presentations during meetings.

senses.definition: a list that presents different options on a computer screen and remains there until one option is chosen by the user

examples.x: To select your preferred language, click on the drop-down menu at the top right corner of the screen.
The drop-down menu allows users to choose their preferred font size for better readability.
In the settings, you can find a drop-down menu to select your time zone.
When filling out the form, use the drop-down menu to indicate your country of residence.
The drop-down menu for sorting emails by date, sender, or subject is located in the inbox toolbar.

senses.definition: someone who uses computers to illegally access someone else's computer or phone

examples.x: The hacker gained unauthorized access to the company's database and stole sensitive information.
Law enforcement agencies are working to identify the hacker responsible for the recent cyberattack.
The hacker installed malware on unsuspecting users' devices to steal their personal information.
Companies invest in cybersecurity measures to protect their systems from hackers.
Hackers often exploit software vulnerabilities to infiltrate computer systems.

senses.definition: a service that gives information and support to the computer users, particularly those who work in the same place

examples.x: The help desk provides technical assistance to employees experiencing computer-related issues.
If you encounter any problems with your computer, you can contact the help desk for assistance.
The help desk staff are trained to troubleshoot software and hardware problems.
Employees can submit support tickets to the help desk for assistance with IT issues.
The help desk offers guidance on software installation and configuration.

senses.definition: (computing) the program through which a user can interact with a computer, especially its design and appearance

examples.x: The interface of the new software is user-friendly and intuitive, making it easy for beginners to navigate.
The graphic designer was responsible for creating the interface for the mobile app, focusing on sleek design and functionality.
Users can customize the interface of their operating system to suit their preferences.
The interface allows users to interact with the computer using a combination of mouse clicks and keyboard shortcuts.
The company conducted usability testing to gather feedback on the interface design before launching the product.

senses.definition: a very small piece of electronic equipment in a computer programed to control all the functions of the CPU

examples.x: The Intel Core i7 is a powerful microprocessor commonly found in high-end desktop computers.
The Raspberry Pi single-board computer utilizes a Broadcom microprocessor to execute various tasks.
Mobile devices such as smartphones and tablets rely on microprocessors like the Qualcomm Snapdragon or Apple A-series chips.
Embedded systems like smart appliances or automotive control units often integrate microprocessors from manufacturers like Texas Instruments or NXP Semiconductors.
Consumer electronics like smart TVs or digital cameras incorporate specialized microprocessors to handle multimedia processing and user interfaces.

senses.definition: the application of images, text, audio, and video files collectively

examples.x: Multimedia presentations engage audiences by incorporating various elements like images, animations, and sound effects.
The website features multimedia content such as videos, slideshows, and interactive graphics.
Educational software often includes multimedia components to enhance learning through visual and auditory stimuli.
Digital marketing campaigns leverage multimedia content to deliver compelling messages across multiple channels.
The multimedia department at the university offers courses in digital media production, graphic design, and audio engineering.

senses.definition: a compact electronic device designed for individual use, capable of performing various tasks such as word processing, internet browsing, and multimedia applications
a desktop computer that is connected to a network, which is more upgraded than a personal computer

examples.x: The graphic designer's workstation is equipped with a high-resolution monitor and powerful graphics card for handling complex design projects.
Engineers rely on specialized workstations with multiple processors and large amounts of memory for running simulations and CAD software.
The finance department uses workstations with advanced processing capabilities to analyze large datasets and generate financial reports.
The IT department manages a network of workstations across the organization, ensuring they are secure, up-to-date, and optimized for performance.
Workstations in research laboratories are equipped with specialized software and peripherals for conducting experiments and analyzing data.

senses.definition: a computer program used for calculating data, especially financial data

examples.x: The accountant used a spreadsheet to track expenses, revenue, and profits for the fiscal year.
Students can use a spreadsheet to organize data and perform calculations for scientific experiments.
The sales team relies on a spreadsheet to forecast sales projections and monitor performance against targets.
The budget spreadsheet includes formulas for automatically calculating totals and generating visual charts.
Engineers use a spreadsheet to perform structural analysis, calculate material requirements, and estimate costs for construction projects.

senses.definition: to use computers to perform a task or do a particular job

examples.x: The company decided to computerize its inventory management system to improve efficiency.
We need to computerize our payroll processing to reduce errors and streamline operations.
The school district is considering options to computerize student attendance tracking for more accurate reporting.
As part of the digital transformation initiative, the retail store plans to computerize inventory tracking and sales reporting.
The restaurant has computerized its ordering system to speed up service and reduce wait times.

senses.definition: to transform data into a coded form

examples.x: The spy agency uses specialized methods to encode classified messages for secure communication.
Barcode scanners are designed to encode information on products for quick identification.
In digital communication, data is often encoded before transmission for error detection and correction.

senses.definition: (computing) to prepare a storage device, such as a hard drive or USB, for use by deleting all the data on it and setting it up for a specific file system

examples.x: I need to format my external hard drive before transferring files to it.
Before selling my old laptop, I need to format the hard drive to erase all my personal data.
To resolve the issue with the USB drive, I had to format it and reinstall the necessary files.
The IT department will format the new external hard drive to ensure compatibility with the company's systems.
Be sure to back up all your files before you format your computer to avoid losing important data.

senses.definition: to move data or a program from a local storage device into the memory of a computer

examples.x: The software engineer needs to load the updated application into the server for testing.
Before starting the presentation, the speaker must load the slideshow into the computer.
IT professionals regularly load security updates and patches to enhance system protection.
Before editing a video, the editor needs to load the raw footage into the video editing software.
The graphic designer will load high-resolution images into the design software for a print project.

senses.definition: to find and collect data stored on a computer

examples.x: The IT specialist was able to retrieve the lost files from the backup server.
Users can retrieve their emails from any device with internet access using webmail services.
It took hours to retrieve the corrupted data from the damaged hard drive.
The software has a feature to retrieve previous versions of documents in case of accidental changes.
The forensic team was able to retrieve deleted files from the suspect's computer.

senses.definition: to improve a machine, computer system, etc. in terms of efficiency, standards, etc.

examples.x: The company upgrades its software regularly to enhance performance.
He upgrades his phone every two years to get the latest features.
The city is upgrading its public transportation infrastructure.
The engineers are upgrading the power grid for better efficiency.
The team has upgraded the website to improve user experience.

senses.definition: to play computer or video games

examples.x: During the weekend, I like to game with my siblings and compete in multiplayer matches.
He prefers to game on his PC because of the superior graphics and performance.
My friends and I often game late into the night, immersed in virtual worlds and epic battles.

senses.definition: a type of computer memory tasked with temporarily storing data for a quicker access
a device fixed in a computer to store information, which retains data if power goes off and is much faster compared to a hard drive

a decentralized naming system used on the Internet to translate human-readable domain names into numerical IP addresses that computers can understand, enabling the proper routing of data between devices and servers
